mission.title: sops: Configurable indentation for yaml output in .sops.yaml
mission.description: Configurable indentation for yaml output in .sops.yaml. Requested by 64+ users.

steps.title: Check current sops deployment
steps.description:
Verify your sops version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n sops -l app.kubernetes.io/name=sops
helm list -n sops 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working sops installation.

steps.title: Review sops configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant sops configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n sops -l app.kubernetes.io/name=sops
kubectl get configmap -n sops -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=sops
```
It would be great if we could configure our indentation for yaml in the .sops.yaml configuration file. Right now it's just hardcoded to set the indent to 4 all the time.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Configurable indentation for yaml output in .sops.yaml
steps.description:
this will allow for setting of parameters specific to each store, such as indentation level for YAML (https://github.com/mozilla/sops/issues/900).
```yaml
yq e -P somefile.yml > somefile.fixed.yml
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n sops -l app.kubernetes.io/name=sops
kubectl get events -n sops --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Configurable indentation for yaml output in .sops.yaml" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: this will allow for setting of parameters specific to each store, such as indentation level for YAML (https://github.com/mozilla/sops/issues/900).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with sops installed or the issue environment reproducible.
